pizza.hero_subtitle: Celebrating the world's first Bitcoin transaction
pizza.story_title: It all started with a pizza
pizza.story_p1: On May 22nd, 2010, a programmer named Laszlo Hanyecz made the first-ever commercial Bitcoin transaction. He paid 10,000 Bitcoin for two pizzas - worth about $41 at the time.
pizza.story_p2: This isn't a story about someone who 'lost' money. It's about someone who bravely used Bitcoin as real money - no bank, no middleman, no permission needed.
pizza.why_1_body: For the first time in history, two people could exchange value without a third party.
pizza.why_2_body: Transactions are recorded forever on the blockchain - no one can delete or forge them.
pizza.why_3_body: No borders, no international wire fees, no one to ask permission from.
pizza.returns_title: If you'd bought 1,000,000 ₫ of Bitcoin on every Pizza Day…
pizza.returns_placeholder_note: Historical data will be populated in the next build phase.

bitcoin.hero_sub: If you understand gold, you can understand Bitcoin.
bitcoin.intro_title: Gold and Bitcoin - the same principle
bitcoin.intro_p1: For thousands of years, Vietnamese people have trusted gold as the best way to protect savings. No one can print more gold. No one can easily fake gold. Gold holds its value across generations.
bitcoin.intro_p2: Bitcoin was built on those same principles - but in digital form. The total supply of Bitcoin is capped at 21 million - no one can create more, not even its creators.
bitcoin.intro_p3: But Bitcoin can do things gold cannot: you can send Bitcoin to a relative overseas in minutes, with no bank and almost no fees.
bitcoin.matrix_caption: 11 criteria · from scarcity to mis-grading risk
bitcoin.matrix_summary: Bitcoin doesn't replace gold - it's a better digital version of it.
bitcoin.solves_title: What does Bitcoin solve for Vietnamese people?
bitcoin.solves_1_body: No Western Union, no bank, no 5–10% fees. Send 1 million or 100 million - same low fee, arriving in minutes.
bitcoin.solves_2_body: Bitcoin has a stronger long-term track record than gold. No one can print more Bitcoin to dilute your savings.
bitcoin.solves_3_body: You don't need to trust a bank, a gold shop, or any government. Bitcoin is yours, held by you.
bitcoin.scam_title: How is Bitcoin different from other crypto?
bitcoin.scam_1: Bitcoin is the first and largest cryptocurrency, born in 2009, with a market cap larger than many country's economies.
bitcoin.scam_2: No company, no CEO, no one in control.
bitcoin.scam_3: The crypto scam stories you have heard in Vietnam involve other crypto projects - not Bitcoin. Bitcoin is not associated with scams.
bitcoin.scam_4: Bitcoin Da Nang only talks about Bitcoin. Our community is not open to other cryptocurrency projects due to the number of scammers.
bitcoin.start_1_body: Join the Bitcoin Da Nang Telegram group to ask questions.
bitcoin.start_2_body: Use BitcoinVN.io or the ATM at Westwood Da Nang.
bitcoin.start_3_body: Come to our monthly meetup at Westwood.

matrix.r1_gold: Naturally limited but can still be mined
matrix.r2_gold: Hard to divide - need to visit a gold shop
matrix.r4_gold: Needs special equipment, can be faked
matrix.r5_gold: Heavy, hard to carry, hard to cross borders
matrix.r6_gold: Needs a safe, risk of theft
matrix.r9_gold: High in Vietnam due to SJC restrictions
